nodes.label: P5.3 — lost/rotated + profiler + truncation floor
nodes.rationale: The residual decay is lost (MLP recovers only 0.005) not rotated; placement value is task-dependent (flat +0.108, headroom ~0 plateau); establishes the truncation floor (0.564) every later READ tier must beat, and flags preservation (P5.6) as low-value.

nodes.label: The truncation floor — the cheap cost baseline every reader must beat
nodes.rationale: A from-scratch short SCFF stack (matched budget, own-tuned) trained at the committed temp/window and read at the top; established at P5.3 as 0.564 (L7 own-tuned) on headroom — the operational floor P5.4/P5.5 must beat before any reader is adopted.

nodes.label: P5.3 verdict: the residual decay is LOST not ROTATED; placement is the READ lever
nodes.rationale: An MLP probe recovers only 0.005 at L12 versus the linear probe — the class information is genuinely destroyed (lost), not merely linearly-inaccessible (rotated); since preservation cannot recover lost information, and the decay is now small (~0.02) at temp0.2, placement (reading the extractor's end) is the READ lever, not objective-surgery.

nodes.label: Placement value is task-dependent: large on easy tasks, ~zero on headroom
nodes.rationale: Reading the extractor's end vs the L12 top gains +0.108 on flat and +0.077 on mixed (deep layers are pure tunnel on easy tasks) but only +0.013 on headroom (a broad plateau L3-L11); the profiler (probe peak) agrees with the readout-optimal on headroom (+/-1 layer) but runs ~2 layers too deep on flat/mixed, so placement must be readout-driven, not probe-driven.
